title: Playtest Closure, Release Gates, Rollback, Recovery, and Production Defense · Transfer Build
summary: An independent adaptive-arcade team receives a different viewport, device, frame rate, seed, entity count, accessibility profile, platform, and delivery constraint and transfers playtest closure, release gates, rollback, recovery, and production defense into a changed production game release and defense dossier. Planned artifact: Produce an independent changed-context version of production game release and defense dossier with automated and judgment evidence. Learners progress through predictio…
objectives: Trace each observed barrier or defect through cause, priority, repair, regression evidence, retest, disposition, and owner.
Gate an immutable identified artifact on correctness, replay, accessibility, performance, portability, security, licenses, and clean launch.
Preserve bounded diagnostics and settings backups, recover corrupted local state safely, and provide accessible support instructions.
Preserve previously learned constraints and attach observable changed-case evidence.

starterFiles.python:
"""build-asteroids-python cumulative accessible real-time game portfolio.

Browser execution uses deterministic pure-Python simulation models and fixed fixtures only.
Native pygame-ce, SDL, windows, controllers, audio devices, assets, files, installers,
GPU behavior, frame pacing, accessibility playtests, packaging, and production effects require controlled transfer gates.
"""

from dataclasses import dataclass


@dataclass(frozen=True)
class SimulationPolicy:
    fixed_step_seconds: float = 1 / 120
    max_frame_seconds: float = 0.1
    max_updates_per_frame: int = 8
    logical_width: int = 960
    logical_height: int = 540


@dataclass(frozen=True)
class RunEvidence:
    revision: str
    seed: int
    input_trace_id: str
    runtime_id: str
    accessibility_profile: str


def portfolio_ready() -> str:
    return "Python Asteroids portfolio ready"

steps.title: Asteroids, pygame-ce, and Python Commit to the mission model · Lab Playtest Closure
steps.instruction:
lab case — A lab Asteroids team handles case 9058 during a keyboard-only community arcade night. Build deterministic Python evidence to defend an immutable game release across player value, accessibility, determinism, performance, portability, security, rollback, recovery, and owned residual risk; retain player goal, revision, Python, pygame-ce, SDL, fixture, seed, input trace, viewport, and result identity; then inject a 400 millisecond frame stall and prove the update budget prevents a spiral of death. Browser code uses pure bounded models and original fixed fixtures only. Real pygame-ce, SDL, windows, controllers, audio devices, assets, files, installers, frame timing, GPUs, packaging, and production behavior require explicit controlled desktop transfer gates. Competency probe: Trace each observed barrier or defect through cause, priority, repair, regression evidence, retest, disposition, and owner.

What must be established before implementation?

Evidence target: Asteroids, pygame-ce, and Python Commit to the mission model · Lab Playtest Closure.
steps.why: The decision supplies observable evidence for trace each observed barrier or defect through cause, priority, repair, regression evidence, retest, disposition, and owner. For Asteroids, pygame-ce, and Python Commit to the mission model · Lab Playtest Closure, keep Playtest Closure connected to executable Python game-state, timing, input, collision, accessibility, test, package, and release decision evidence.

Browser code uses pure bounded models and original fixed fixtures only. Real pygame-ce, SDL, windows, controllers, audio devices, assets, files, installers, frame timing, GPUs, packaging, and production behavior require explicit controlled desktop transfer gates. Competency probe: Trace each observed barrier or defect through cause, priority, repair, regression evidence, retest, disposition, and owner.

steps.title: Asteroids, pygame-ce, and Python Extend the cumulative artifact · Lab Production Defense
steps.instruction:
Append a runnable pure-Python function headed "# Evidence: asteroids-production-defense-ryh1t6" that uses case 6357 to defend an immutable game release across player value, accessibility, determinism, performance, portability, security, rollback, recovery, and owned residual risk. Keep its defaults runnable, assert an invariant, and return observable changed-case evidence. Browser code must not claim native pygame-ce, SDL, window, controller, mixer, filesystem, package, GPU, installer, or production behavior; verify those boundaries later with Python 3.14.6, released pygame-ce 2.5.7, fixed and headless SDL fixtures, real devices, licensed assets, accessibility playtests, profiling, clean installations, rollback, recovery, and release gates.

Evidence target: Asteroids, pygame-ce, and Python Extend the cumulative artifact · Lab Production Defense.
steps.why: Building a runnable or mechanically reviewable artifact turns Production Defense into observable performance evidence. For Asteroids, pygame-ce, and Python Extend the cumulative artifact · Lab Production Defense, keep Production Defense connected to executable Python game-state, timing, input, collision, accessibility, test, package, and release decision evidence.

content.code:
# Evidence: asteroids-production-defense-ryh1t6
# Competency: asteroids-production-defense.
# Case 6357: an offline cross-platform release rehearsal.
# Operating constraint: preserve explicit pixels, seconds, radians or degrees, frames, entity counts, collision units, and budgets.
# Changed case: replace an audio-only collision cue with synchronized text, shape, and optional sound evidence.
def evidence_asteroids_production_defense_ryh1t6(correctness: bool = True, accessibility: bool = True, performance: bool = True, portability: bool = True, security: bool = True, rollback: bool = True, recovery: bool = True, residual_risk_owned: bool = True) -> tuple[bool, int | float, str]:
    evidence_variant_ryh1t6 = "ryh1t6-6t1hyr-6357"
    assert evidence_variant_ryh1t6
    evidence_axes_ryh1t6 = (
        "playerryh1t6",
        "seed6357",
        "input6t1hyr",
        "frameryh1t66357",
        "state6357ryh1t6",
        "playtest6t1hyr6357",
    )
    assert len(set(evidence_axes_ryh1t6)) == 6
    gates = (correctness, accessibility, performance, portability, security, rollback, recovery)
    passed = sum(1 for gate in gates if gate)
    if not correctness or not accessibility:
        return False, passed, "player-quality"
    if not performance or not portability or not security:
        return False, passed, "release-quality"
    if not rollback or not recovery:
        return False, passed, "recovery-gap"
    if not residual_risk_owned:
        return False, passed, "unowned-risk"
    assert passed == len(gates)
    return True, passed, "production-game-defense"

steps.title: Asteroids, pygame-ce, and Python Test an acceptance boundary · Lab Production Defense
steps.instruction:
lab case — A lab Asteroids team handles case 5106 during a controller-remapping accessibility review. Build deterministic Python evidence to defend an immutable game release across player value, accessibility, determinism, performance, portability, security, rollback, recovery, and owned residual risk; test empty, zero-vector, seam, low-frame-rate, focus-loss, device-loss, missing-asset, corrupted-save, and rejected cases; then inject a 400 millisecond frame stall and prove the update budget prevents a spiral of death. Browser code uses pure bounded models and original fixed fixtures only. Real pygame-ce, SDL, windows, controllers, audio devices, assets, files, installers, frame timing, GPUs, packaging, and production behavior require explicit controlled desktop transfer gates. Competency probe: Defend player value, game rules, determinism, accessibility, performance, portability, security, support, ownership, and residual risk together.
